Motion To Bifurcate Joint Employer And Punitive Damages Issues (Motions)
Off Calendar Apr-22-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MARSH AND MCCLENNAN COMPANIES, INC., MARSH USA INC. and ARCELI HARVEY'S Motion To Bifurcate Joint Employer And Punitive Damages Issues.

Defendants' motion to bifurcate is denied without prejudice; our court's practice is to defer to the trial judge on such motions.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/SRB)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-21-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tyson Gibb, Esq. (310.888.7771; tyson@setarehlaw.com) of Setareh Law Group for Plaintiff via zoom video conference.
Michael J. Hui, Esq. (415-216-1234; mhui@littler.com) and Laura Tovar, Esq. (415.677.3121; ltovar@littler.com) of Littler Mendelson PC for Defendant.

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS MARSH AND MCCLENNAN COMPANIES, INC., MARSH USA INC., and ARCELI HARVEY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Defendants Marsh USA, Marsh & McLennan Companies Inc. ("MMC") and Arceli Harvey ("Harvey") (collectively, "Defendants"), motion for summary judgment of Plaintiff Lakeysha Kaufman's complaint is denied. Defendants motion for summary adjudication is denied in part and granted in part.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
